AoE Templates
Place circles, cones, lines, squares, and cubes for spells. Click once to set the origin, click again to lock it. Right-click a template to remove it.

Common presets are provided for fast D&D placement. Free Size is still available for custom templates.

Journal: campaign notes, lore, NPC/location references, and shared quest briefs.
Model: Quests = objectives, Discoveries = clue reveals, Handouts = DM documents, Moments = quick timeline beats.

Category

✦ Discovery Cards ▼
Discoveries are clue drops — short reveals for what someone just noticed, uncovered, or learned. Use Journal for longer canonical notes.
No queued discoveries yet.
📝 DM Notes ▼
Private notes for the DM only — never broadcast to players. Auto-saved to the campaign.
🕯 Private Prompts & Objectives ▼
Send an immersive private prompt or a persistent roleplay objective to a single player. These never broadcast to the full party.
No private prompts or objectives recorded yet.

Fog of War
Fog Mode
Enable Fog
Advanced Actions
Editing: World Map
Fog is OFF · players can see the full map.
Visibility model: Off = unrestricted, Manual = DM-painted fog, Vision = token LOS, Hybrid = both manual + LOS.
Brush Size: 3
Advanced Fog Notes
Brush and shape tools work on touch + mouse. In Hybrid mode, revealed areas remain explored while active line-of-sight stays brightest.
🔊 Ambient Sound
Keep the room’s mood steady here. Ambient and narration are the primary live-play controls; the rest can stay tucked away until needed.
Ambient: Silence
Master Vol 80%
Ambient Vol 70%
SFX Vol 100%
⚔ Sound Effects One-shot cues
Use for sharp beats during combat, reveals, and reactions without changing the room’s ambient loop.
📜 Narration
Write a short beat, preview the voice, then broadcast when you are happy with the tone.
🌦 Weather Scene dressing
Use weather when the table needs a stronger visual mood pass. Keep it collapsed when you are not actively tuning the scene.

      Encounter Draft ▶
      Build a reusable encounter from the live bestiary, then spawn the whole group onto the current map.
      Open a creature from the bestiary and use Add to Encounter to build a reusable draft, then save it as a template or spawn the whole group on the active map.

      These live in the current campaign state and stay in sync for the DM.

      ✦ Character Forge
      —
      —
      Saved
      Character Sheet
      This page is no longer part of the normal character flow.
      Notes
      Player notes only. Species details now live on the main character sheet tab.
      Vitals & Core Stats
      Use this page to confirm HP, AC, speed, initiative, proficiency bonus, passive perception, and ability scores.
      Current HP
      Live HP to place on the token.
      Max HP
      Used for token placement and updates.
      Armor Class
      Shield, armor, magical buffs, or custom AC.
      Speed
      Used by combat movement limits.
      Ability Scores
      STR
      +0
      DEX
      +0
      CON
      +0
      INT
      +0
      WIS
      +0
      CHA
      +0
      Combat Basics
      Saving Throws & Skills
      You can keep the exact numbers from the D&D Beyond sheet here. Imported values stay editable, so you can correct anything the parser misses.
      Saving Throws
      Skills
      Actions
      Weapons, actions, proficiencies, languages, and the rules-facing details you want on hand at the table.
      Weapons & Attacks
      Actions
      Gear, Proficiencies & Language
      Parsed Weapons & Attacks
      Each line above becomes a quick roll card with attack bonus, range, and damage dice.
      Native Action Library
      Structured class actions, bonus actions, and reactions from the native runtime appear here.
      Class Resources
      Tracked pools like Rage, Focus Points, Bardic Inspiration, Channel Divinity, and similar resources show here when native data is available.
      Senses
      Resistances & Immunities
      Vulnerabilities
      Magic
      A clean place for the longer rule text from D&D Beyond — class features, feats, spell notes, spell save DC, and spell attack bonus.
      Spellcasting
      Features & Traits
      Feats & Boons
      Spells & Notes
      Parsed Spellbook Preview
      Imported PDFs and JSON exports render here as a clean scrollable reference list so spells are easier to read during play.
      Rules-Linked Spellbook
      Matches imported spell names to the structured rules database and calculates scaling from your current character stats.
      Native Features & Traits
      Structured passive features and class unlocks from the native runtime appear here so the sheet is not relying only on pasted text.
      Import & Conversion
      Import a character from D&D Beyond or another source, then clean it up in Notes and Sheet.
      Quick Paste Import
      This parser handles the most common pasted sheet fields first, then leaves the rest editable for cleanup.
      D&D Beyond JSON / PDF
      After Import
      After importing, check your identity details, confirm HP / AC / speed on the Vitals page, look over skills and saves, then use Apply To Quick Panel to keep the token fields in sync.
      Use this once after a character import if you want their PDF gear list and coin totals copied into the Inventory tab.
      Inventory & Loadout
      Review equipped weapons, ammo, carried gear, and coin totals here before combat. This page should be your quick loadout check.
      Carried Items
      Guided Level Up
      Use this after the Sheet, Actions, and Magic pages feel correct. It is the progression step, not the first place to fix bad source data.
      Step 1 — Summary
      Current class/level snapshot with required choices highlighted.
      Step 2 — Automatic Gains
      HP, slot progression, feature unlocks, and class resource caps with before/after diffs.
      Step 3 — Choices
      Only required choices for this level (ASI/feat, spell choices, subclass options, etc.).
      Step 4 — Review & Confirm
      Final change summary and persistence through existing profile/update pathways.
      Spell Library ✦
      Spells granted by your DM appear here with a gold border. Use the search and filter controls to find spells. Toggle "Prepare" to mark which spells you have prepared.
      No spells in your library yet. Ask your DM to grant you spells!
      Spell Rules Review
      DM-only workspace for unmatched imported spells and custom spell entries. Players can view linked spell cards but cannot edit rules data.
      Review Queue & Custom Catalog
      Custom Spell Editor
      This custom catalog is DM-only. Players can only read rules already linked to their character.
